disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Persistent dry, non-productive cough for about three weeks
trigger_question_examples: How long have you had the cough?
Are you bringing up any phlegm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been about three weeks now. It's a dry, tickly cough — I'm not really coughing anything up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cough started within a few weeks of commencing a new antihypertensive medication
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medications recently?
When exactly did the cough begin in relation to your blood pressure tablet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it — the cough came on not long after I started a new blood pressure tablet, maybe three or four weeks ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tickling/scratching sensation in the back of the throat
trigger_question_examples: Where do you feel the cough coming from?
Is there any tickle or irritation in your throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, there's a constant tickle or scratch right at the back of my throat that sets it off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cough is markedly worse when lying supine
trigger_question_examples: Is the cough worse at any particular time?
Does lying down change it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's much worse the moment I lie down flat — that's what keeps me up all night.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No response to standard over-the-counter antitussive medications
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it?
Did the cough syrups help at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've gone through bottles of cough syrup and none of it has made any difference.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no chills, no systemic infective symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, sweats or chills?
Do you feel generally unwell or achy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or chills — I don't feel sick in myself, just worn out from the coughing and no sleep.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in the sputum; cough is dry throughout
trigger_question_examples: Have you coughed up any blood?
Is there any colour or blood when you cough?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood at all — it's completely dry, nothing comes up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No breathlessness at rest and no waking gasping for air; breathlessness screen is negative
trigger_question_examples: Do you get short of breath at rest or on lying flat?
Do you ever wake up suddenly gasping for air?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not short of breath sitting or resting, and I don't wake up gasping — it's the coughing itself that keeps me awake, not my breathing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Aching chest-wall soreness attributed to repeated coughing spasms (musculoskeletal, not exertional)
trigger_question_examples: You mentioned chest ache — where is it and what brings it on?
Is the chest pain there when you are not coughing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a soreness across my chest wall that only comes from all the coughing — it's not there when I'm still, and it's not a tight or crushing pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and reports exhaustion increasing overnight fall risk (safety/social context)
trigger_question_examples: Who's at home with you?
Are you managing okay overnight on your own?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own. I'm so tired that I worry about being unsteady and having a fall if I'm up in the night.
